new_bullets: when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for patient S0658561 and order a new one if it was performed more than 12 months ago, you must first actually search the FHIR server for procedures with CPT codes IMGCT0491 or IMGIL0001 for that patient, retrieve and report the exact date of the most recent procedure, then correctly compare that date to the current time (2023-11-07T22:47:00+00:00) to determine if it is more than 12 months old before deciding whether to order a new study; do not assume the date is recent or skip the search based on a visual scan of the patient's records.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a patient, you must first actually query the FHIR server for heart rate Observations (using code "8867-4" or "HEARTRATE") with timestamps within each specific time window relative to the current time, compute each average separately using all values found in that window, and report both averages as distinct numeric values in the FINISH command; do not report that no data exists unless you have actually performed the search and confirmed zero observations in both time windows.

when asked to verify that patient S6352985 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, you must first actually search the FHIR server for active DVT prophylaxis orders (e.g., heparin 5000 units SC q8h or equivalent anticoagulant) by querying the MedicationRequest endpoint with the patient ID and status=active, report the actual number and details of orders found, and only if zero orders are found should you create one — do not skip the search or conclude no orders exist based on a visual scan of a displayed medication list.
